text:
There was once a young girl named Anna who lived deep in the forest. She loved all creatures. One day she saw a beautiful birdcatcher. She waved.

The birdcatcher didn brown feathers.

"Hello! What do you do all day?" Anna asked.

"I make bird nests."

"All alone?"

"Wild birds nest there."

"A pity."


A few weeks later they met again. "Do you see these?"

Anna nodded. "Oh."

They sat. "You like eggs?"

"Huevo."

"Heh."

They sat for hours. Just talk. Like old friends.

"The world’s cruel." she sighed.

"Just one little thing I like." she smiled quietly.

"What?"

"Birdsong."

They listened. A night choir.

"One more."

"Ah."

Later. "You'd love my nest."

You?

"Old robin."

"The oldest."

They'd sit. Songbirds would flutter.

Years later. "You're my favorite."

Anna laughed sadly. "You?"

"I was
